comments.text: A. Chattopadhyay and C. Chattopadhyay in [ChCh26] has shown that:\[\sum^\infty_{k=1}\frac{1}{F_{n^k}}\]is irrational for any integers $n\geq2$

Note that this special case is significantly easier to establish irrationality for than general cases, because of the well known divisibility relation that $F_n | F_m$ when $n|m$, so each denominator here is a multiple of the previous. So this series is similar to the series $e = \sum_{n=1}^\infty \frac{1}{n!}$ for the purposes of establishing irrationality.
The transcendence version of this problem has been solved by K. D. Nguyen in [Ng20]. Also, this paper [Mc94] seems relevant.

FWIW, Badea in [Ba87] also provides a proof of the irrationality of $\sum_{n\geq 1}\frac{1}{F_{2^n + 1}}$ and $\sum_{n\geq 1}\frac{1}{L_{2^n}}$. Badea later gave a proof in [Ba93] of this problem for $c\geq 2$. I believe it remains open if it is true for $c\in(1,2)$. Hančl in [Ha96] generalised Badea's original main result. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
